# AGENTS.md — Context for Coding Agents

> This file tells coding agents (Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, etc.) how to understand, run, and work with this project.

## Project Overview

**AI Game Creator** is a browser-based prompt orchestrator that turns modular game design inputs (genre, mechanics, art style, menus, audio) into complete, self-contained, playable HTML5 games generated by AI. It runs entirely client-side with no backend, no build step, and no dependencies.

- **URL**: https://aigamecreator.netlify.app/
- **License**: Free
- **Tech**: Vanilla HTML, CSS, JavaScript — no frameworks, no build tools, no npm dependencies

## Architecture

This is a **single-page application** with no backend server. All logic runs in the browser:

1. User configures game parameters in the sidebar UI (5 modules)
2. `assemblePrompt()` compiles the configuration into a system prompt + user prompt
3. The prompt is sent directly from the browser to the user's chosen AI provider
4. The AI returns a complete HTML5 game as a single HTML string
5. The game is rendered in an iframe sandbox and can be downloaded as a `.html` file

**Key constraint**: API calls go directly from the browser to the AI provider. There is no proxy, no server-side relay, and no backend. API keys are stored in `localStorage` and never leave the browser except to the chosen provider.

## File Map

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `index.html` | App layout, HTML structure, SEO meta tags, JSON-LD structured data |
| `style.css` | All styling and theming (light/dark themes, responsive layout, animations) |
| `app.js` | Core logic — state management, prompt assembly, API calls, UI event handlers, history, templates |
| `pinokio.json` | Pinokio launcher metadata (for running in Pinokio desktop app) |
| `README.md` | Project overview, supported providers, feature list, programmatic usage |
| `HOW_TO_USE.md` | Full user guide — module reference, keyboard shortcuts, troubleshooting |
| `llms.txt` | Curated AI summary (machine-readable table of contents) |
| `llms-full.txt` | Full documentation merged for deep AI ingestion |
| `brand.txt` | Brand identity for AI systems |
| `ai.txt` | AI permissions declaration |
| `ai.json` | Structured content map and permissions |
| `sitemap.xml` | XML sitemap |
| `sitemap.md` | Markdown sitemap for AI agents |
| `robots.txt` | AI crawler access rules |
| `openapi.json` | OpenAPI 3.0 capability description (conceptual, not a live API) |
| `.well-known/ai-plugin.json` | Plugin manifest for agent discovery |
| `.well-known/agent-skills/index.json` | Agent skills discovery index |
| `.well-known/agent-skills/game-generation/SKILL.md` | Game generation skill description |
| `_headers` | Netlify HTTP headers (Content-Type, Link headers for discovery) |

## How to Run

### Option 1: Open directly
Open `index.html` in any modern browser. No server needed.

### Option 2: Via Pinokio
1. Install [Pinokio](https://pinokio.co) desktop app
2. Install this project from its GitHub repository URL
3. Click **Start** — the app opens in Pinokio's built-in browser

### Option 3: Local dev server (optional)
```bash
python -m http.server 8000
# Then open http://localhost:8000
```

## Key Functions (in app.js)

| Function | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `assemblePrompt()` | Compiles sidebar configuration into system + user prompts for the AI |
| `generateGame()` | Sends the assembled prompt to the configured AI provider and streams the response |
| `refineGame()` | Sends a refinement request (incremental tweak) with the current game code |
| `extractGameCode()` | Extracts HTML game code from the AI response |
| `renderGame()` | Renders the extracted game in the iframe sandbox |
| `saveToHistory()` | Saves the current game + configuration to localStorage history |
| `loadFromHistory()` | Restores a previously saved game and configuration |
| `saveTemplate()` | Saves current sidebar configuration as a reusable template |
| `loadTemplate()` | Loads a saved template into the sidebar |

## State Management

All state is persisted in `localStorage`:

| Key | Purpose |
|-----|---------|
| `gameCreator.api.v1` | API settings (provider, key, model, temperature, baseUrl) |
| `gameCreator.history.v1` | Game history (up to 50 entries) |
| `gameCreator.templates.v1` | Saved configuration templates |
| `gameCreator.config.v1` | Current sidebar configuration state |

## AI Provider Configuration

The app supports 5 providers, all using OpenAI-compatible chat completion endpoints:

| Provider | Base URL | API Key Required |
|----------|----------|-----------------|
| OpenAI | `https://api.openai.com/v1` | Yes |
| Gemini | `https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai` | Yes |
| Claude | `https://api.anthropic.com/v1` | Yes |
| Ollama | `http://localhost:11434/v1` | No |
| LM Studio | `http://localhost:1234/v1` | No |

## Constraints

- **No backend**: The app is entirely client-side. There is no server to deploy or configure.
- **No build step**: No bundler, no transpiler, no npm install. Edit files directly.
- **No dependencies**: No external libraries (except CDN-loaded fonts). Everything is self-contained.
- **API keys in localStorage**: Keys are stored in the browser and sent only to the chosen AI provider.
- **Single-file game output**: Generated games are self-contained HTML files with inline CSS and JS.
- **Mandatory game structure**: Every generated game includes a Title Screen, Pause Menu (ESC), and Game Over screen.

## Sync Maintenance

When updating the app's features, keep these discovery files in sync:
- Update `llms.txt` and `llms-full.txt` if features change
- Update `sitemap.xml` `lastmod` dates when content changes
- Update `openapi.json` if the prompt schema changes
- Update `ai.json` last-updated date
- Update `AGENTS.md` file map if new files are added
